claim: Lantern can cache Googled info from other LLMs as compressed source-tagged claims, not raw dumps.
compressedSummary: Create a filtered RAG intake lane for external LLM/web research with source URLs, dates, rights state, evidence class, confidence, and decision fields.

claim: Itch should be first public store lane before Steam or GOG for early Lantern demos.
compressedSummary: Official itch butler docs support pushing project directories to channels. For Lantern, this makes Itch a lower-friction first public store lane for HTML/ZIP demos before paid Steam app fees or curated GOG review.

claim: Tony/Ultron/Vision notes define a team-boundary warning: stop trying alone and use release gates before powerful systems.
compressedSummary: Operator image note says not to make Ultron, that Tony needed to let go, and that trying alone is boring. RAG use: treat Avengers/Jarvis/Ultron/Vision as a design metaphor for team review, release gates, and anti-single-operator overreach, not as factual proof.

topic: operator field notes - sensitive research domains
claim: Unclassified geopolitics, weapons, cures, UFOs, and inferred-drives notes require verification and safety gates before research or action.
compressedSummary: Operator image note asks for broad coverage across China, UK, NATO, weapons, cures, UFOs, inertial drives, and novel movements. RAG use: create research lanes only with lawful source checking, no operational harmful detail, no medical claims, and no unsupported geopolitical assertions.

claim: Convergence with resistant people needs explicit agreements, work/home/sleep boundaries, and independent research coverage.
compressedSummary: Operator image note asks how to deal with stubborn people, how not to make agreements as incubator/founder, asks for boundaries, and mentions needing two researchers for full data coverage. RAG use: boundary-first collaboration, scheduled availability, consent, and coverage redundancy.

topic: operator field notes - protected creative art
claim: Protected family creative drawings can inform private tone, game-world cues, and education packets, but not public identity or raw-data release.
compressedSummary: Operator-provided drawings include game-like characters and Kingdom/Hearts language. RAG use: summarize as private creative/style signal for game, art, and family learning lanes; do not publish child/private identity, infer psychology, or store as public proof.

topic: operator field notes - audio and consensus
claim: Frank Sinatra audio narration and PBFT-style consensus should become long-form implementation notes with multi-operator voting and cascade-failure prevention.
compressedSummary: Operator image note references Frank Sinatra audio narration, PBFT consensus, 2/3+1 voting, primary rotation, consensus ledger, and preventing single-operator cascade failure. RAG use: candidate implementation plan for narration plus operator consensus, pending code validation.

topic: operator field notes - server farm economics
claim: Continuous server use and coin mining must be routed through power, heat, hardware, legal, and ROI checks before any action.
compressedSummary: Operator note says run continuous server farm, mining coins, and power/electricity is a cap/cost assessment. RAG use: server-farm room must inventory nodes and estimate power/cooling/payback before mining or always-on compute.

topic: operator field notes - friends partners belief fit
claim: Friend, collaborator, and life-partner discovery should use belief-system fit and mutual consent, not money alone.
compressedSummary: Operator note asks for finding friends and life partners using Bayesian world model and belief systems regardless of money. RAG use: candidate social graph and matching framework with consent, privacy, and no profiling without permission.

topic: operator field notes - Buffett Jarvis cash discipline
claim: Buffett-style cash discipline plus Jarvis-style mapping should route cash to reserves, productive assets, public portfolio display, and anti-sprawl hobbies.
compressedSummary: Operator note references Warren Buffett, Jarvis map idea, holding cash, small wild crypto/local farm allocation, per-check investing, public salary/staff-level portfolio display, and hobbies versus speculation. RAG use: financial planning prompt template only, not investment advice.

topic: operator field notes - phone edge node
claim: The phone is an edge capture/control node with no hidden features or secret collection.
compressedSummary: Operator-provided phone images connect the physical phone to the Lantern edge-node lane. RAG use: mobile capture/control, approvals, and viewing; no hidden features, no private collection, no unsafe handling, and no claim of phone dual boot without device-specific proof.

claim: Foundry incubator starts with a human resources audit, PC-first Windows-only path, no hidden features, free media/text/video lanes, store mass connector, and founder rails.
compressedSummary: Operator note says create incubator for foundry, audit human resources first, PC first Windows only, no hidden features, first 24 update offers, free music/audio/text/video, store mass connector, help/train data pool, train founder pool, and self-help with founder rails. RAG use: candidate build plan.

claim: Spring Valley Wildlife Area marker provides a public local ecology learning anchor for wetlands, birds, and place-based observation.
compressedSummary: Operator image shows Ohio Historical Marker for Spring Valley Wildlife Area. RAG use: public place note for ecology, wetlands, birds, local learning, and field observation; no private location inference beyond the public marker content.

topic: operator pasted GitHub repo state - dependency graph
claim: GitHub dependency graph showed no dependencies because Lantern OS lacks a supported dependency manifest at that surface.
compressedSummary: Operator pasted GitHub dependency graph text: no dependencies found; GitHub requires supported manifest files such as package.json or Gemfile. RAG use: add dependency-manifest check before claiming supply-chain coverage.

topic: operator pasted GitHub repo state - workflow token format
claim: The reviewed workflows appeared safe for GitHub token format changes because they use standard Actions token handling and no hardcoded token-length validation.
compressedSummary: Operator pasted another agent summary saying static-surface-ci.yml and release-provenance.yml were reviewed and use standard permissions/automatic token handling. RAG use: candidate validation note; verify against local workflow files before final release claim.

claim: Central RAG book files were reported ready: central index, RAG manifest, multi-repo consolidation, maintenance guide, and deployment checklist.
compressedSummary: Operator pasted a status block listing books/CENTRAL-BOOK-INDEX.md, books/rag-manifest.json, books/MULTI-REPO-CONSOLIDATION.md, books/BOOK-MAINTENANCE.md, and books/DEPLOYMENT-CHECKLIST.md with a proposed consolidation commit. RAG use: find or create these files before claiming completion.

topic: operator pasted repo settings - PR and release gates
claim: Repo settings pasted by operator emphasize master as default branch, PR merge modes, release immutability, branch cleanup, issues/discussions/projects, and signoff toggles.
compressedSummary: Operator pasted GitHub repository settings text. RAG use: turn into release-governance checklist only after checking actual repo settings through GitHub/API; do not assume toggles loaded from pasted UI text are current.

claim: All streamable chat interfaces should store conversations locally with explicit boundaries and no secret/public leak.
compressedSummary: Operator explicitly requested local conversation storage. Implemented Garage app local JSONL conversation API and UI panel; RAG use: conversations are local operator memory, ignored from git, retrievable through /api/conversations, not public release material.

claim: Frank Sinatra/radio/audio narration should converge across local PC app, web app, and streamable chat interfaces as a tone and media-control lane.
compressedSummary: Operator pasted that Frank Sinatra was playing on the local PC app and asked that web app and streamable chat interfaces converge. RAG use: candidate audio lane with local files/rights checks, user controls, and no copyrighted redistribution without permission.

claim: Session limits and failed sends require local handoff packets so Lantern can resume without losing operator state.
compressedSummary: Operator pasted session-limit and retry messages. RAG use: prioritize local handoff/conversation storage and compressed state packets so failed cloud sessions do not strand current work.

claim: 1@5/26 records a bounded Lantern OS control-plane test over repos, docs, skills, MCP-visible boundaries, RAG cache, conversations, and UI gates.
compressedSummary: Operator asked for !cometshot !oneshot 100 times as a record reference. Bounded execution is one validated pass, not destructive repetition. Yes: read-only flat ingestion and UI refresh. No: repo deletion, boot mutation, hidden features, or unverified claims.
